q.en: According to the USDA, about how many American children lived in food-insecure households in 2014?
q.zh: 根据美国农业部数据，2014年约有多少美国儿童生活在粮食无保障的家庭？

options.en: 48.1 million according to some versions of the story

explain.en: The book states 15.3 million children were food insecure—about one in five U.S. children.
explain.zh: 书中说明有1530万儿童粮食无保障，约占美国儿童的五分之一。

q.en: In what year was the Food Stamp Program renamed SNAP?
q.zh: “食品券计划”是在哪一年更名为SNAP（补充营养援助计划）的？

options.en: 1939 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: In 2008 the Food Stamp Program was renamed the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP).
explain.zh: 2008年，食品券计划更名为“补充营养援助计划”（SNAP）。

options.en: A place with too many grocery stores according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 杂货店过多的地方（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A desert where food grows wild
A high-poverty area not served by large grocery stores
options.zh: 没有大型杂货店服务的高贫困区域

explain.en: The book defines food deserts as high-poverty areas not served by large grocery stores.
explain.zh: 书中将食物荒漠定义为没有大型杂货店服务的高贫困区域。

q.en: Which organization is described as the largest nonprofit helping American families, with more than 200 food banks?
q.zh: 哪个组织被描述为帮助美国家庭的最大非营利机构，拥有200多个食物银行？

options.en: The Red Cross according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 红十字会（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Feeding America has more than 200 food banks and 60,000 pantries feeding about 12 million kids a year.
explain.zh: “喂饱美国”拥有200多个食物银行和6万个食品分发点，每年为约1200万儿童提供食物。

q.en: The book opens with a story about a boy named Mario. What is the author's purpose for this opening?
q.zh: 本书以一个叫马里奥的男孩的故事开头。作者这样开头的目的是什么？

options.en: To teach a math lesson according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 上一堂数学课（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To put a human face on hunger and engage the reader
options.zh: 让饥饿问题具象化并吸引读者

explain.en: Mario's story illustrates the daily reality of childhood hunger in the U.S.
explain.zh: 马里奥的故事展现了美国儿童日常面临的饥饿现实。

q.en: The glossary defines 'outstripped' as 'was larger... than; exceeded.' How does the book use this word?
q.zh: 词汇表将“outstripped”解释为“大于；超过”。书中如何使用这个词？

options.en: It says rising costs have outstripped most people's wages
options.zh: 书中说上涨的开支超过了多数人的工资

options.en: It says children outstripped adults according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 儿童超过了成人（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The text says since the 1950s, rising costs of housing, medical care, and food have outstripped wages.
explain.zh: 文中说自20世纪50年代以来，住房、医疗和食品上涨的开支超过了人们的工资。

q.en: The book connects the Healthy, Hunger-Free Kids Act of 2010 to school lunches. What does this show?
q.zh: 书中将《2010年健康无饥饿儿童法案》与学校午餐联系起来。这说明了什么？

options.en: That government action improved nutrition standards for low-income students
options.zh: 政府行动改善了低收入学生的营养标准

options.en: That schools stopped serving lunch according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 学校停止供应午餐（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: With Michelle Obama's urging, Congress passed the act, increasing funding and setting new nutrition standards.
explain.zh: 在米歇尔·奥巴马的推动下，国会通过该法案，增加拨款并制定了新的营养标准。

q.en: The text notes charities account for less than 6 percent of U.S. food assistance. What conclusion can be drawn?
q.zh: 文中指出慈善机构在美国粮食援助中占比不足6%。可以得出什么结论？

options.en: Government programs provide the vast majority, with charities filling a smaller gap
options.zh: 政府项目提供了绝大部分援助，慈善机构只是填补较小缺口

options.en: Charities provide most food assistance according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 慈善机构提供了大部分粮食援助（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Feeding America and similar groups help, but government programs supply the bulk of assistance.
explain.zh: “喂饱美国”等机构有帮助，但政府项目提供了援助的主体。

q.en: The focus question asks why there is an 'epidemic of hunger' in the U.S. and how kids can help. What is the author's main purpose?
q.zh: 焦点问题问：为何美国会出现饥饿“流行病”，以及孩子能如何帮忙？作者的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To describe foreign countries only according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 只描述外国（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To inform readers about U.S. hunger and inspire them to act
options.zh: 让读者了解美国的饥饿问题并激励他们行动

explain.en: The book explains the causes of hunger and highlights what kids can do to help.
explain.zh: 本书解释了饥饿的成因，并强调孩子可以如何提供帮助。

q.en: The book highlights individual kids like William Winslow who collected thousands of pounds of food. What message does the author want to convey?
q.zh: 书中着重描写了威廉·温斯洛等收集了数千磅食物的个别孩子。作者想传达什么信息？

options.en: That ordinary kids can make a real difference in fighting hunger
options.zh: 普通孩子也能在对抗饥饿中带来真正的改变

options.en: That food collection is impossible according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 收集食物是不可能的（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: William says, 'Any kid can make a difference if they want to.'
explain.zh: 威廉说：“任何孩子只要愿意，都能带来改变。”

prompt.en: The book says about 40 percent of U.S. food is thrown away. What is one way your family could waste less food, and why would it matter?
prompt.zh: 书中说美国约40%的食物被丢弃。你的家庭可以如何减少食物浪费？这为何重要？

en: Describe one practical habit to reduce waste.
Explain who benefits from wasting less food.
zh: 描述一个减少浪费的实用习惯。
说明减少浪费能让谁受益。
